title: Simple Siamese Model with Long Short-Term Memory for User Authentication with Field-Programmable Gate Arrays

biometric.body_location: right arm (palmaris longus and extensor digitorum)

method.training_strategy: Categorical cross-entropy loss with Adam and AdaGrad optimizers, Bayesian optimization via Keras Tuner

evaluation.context: Achieved on training data from 80 individuals

evaluation_protocol: 80 subjects used for training/validation (25% split) and 20 subjects used for independent testing.

reported_limitations: slightly slower processing speed due to limited hardware resources
need for further integration and optimization for practical applications

usability.usability_issues: Processing speed is slightly slower on FPGA (7ms) compared to CPU (2.5ms).

key_contribution: Proposed SSiamese-LSTM architecture to minimize hardware resource consumption while maintaining high authentication accuracy
Implementation of EMG biometric authentication on FPGA for edge devices
main_strength: Extremely low parameter count (17,268) suitable for wearable ICs
High reported accuracy (99%+) on the tested dataset
main_weakness: Lack of evaluation in uncontrolled, real-world settings
insights.notes_for_review: The authors claim 'non-aware authentication' in the abstract, but the methodology relies on 'fist-clenching', which is an explicit user action.
